card.intuition: An object storage system designed for massive scaling, durability, and accessibility of unstructured data like images, videos, and backups.
card.analogy: A giant warehouse with bins: instead of a filing cabinet where folders are inside other folders (Traditional FS), you just have a 'Key' (the bin number) and you can put an 'Object' (the item) inside. The warehouse can house billions of bins without getting slower.
when_to_use: Storing user-uploaded profile pictures and videos
Serving static assets (JS, CSS, HTML) for a website
Storing logs and database backups for disaster recovery
gotchas: Consistency: object stores often use 'Eventual Consistency' for overwrites. If you update a file and immediately read it, you might still get the old version.
Direct Uploads: don't route large file uploads through your application server. Use 'Pre-signed URLs' so the client can upload directly to storage.

exercise.prompt: Identify the technique where a file is split into smaller chunks (e.g., 64MB) and distributed across multiple servers for parallel access and redundancy.
